tech-ai.title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI has hired Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball as it prepares for an initial public offering. The moves signal the company is bolstering its leadership and expertise ahead of a major market transition.

tech-ai.title: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips
tech-ai.summary: Amazon Web Services is in advanced discussions to sell its custom AI chips to other data centers, a move CEO Andy Jassy estimates could represent a $50 billion market opportunity. This strategy would position AWS as a direct competitor to Nvidia in the lucrative AI infrastructure market.

tech-ai.title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
tech-ai.summary: AI infrastructure startup Baseten is nearing completion of a $1.5 billion funding round at a $13 billion valuation, capitalizing on intense investor interest in the AI inference space. The rapid fundraising reflects growing demand for AI model deployment and optimization technologies.

tech-ai.title: Source: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI for up to $85M
tech-ai.summary: Infrastructure software company Elastic has agreed to acquire AI-powered bug detection startup DeductiveAI for up to $85 million, expanding its capabilities in automated software testing and debugging. The acquisition demonstrates enterprise software vendors' growing focus on integrating AI-driven quality assurance tools.

tech-ai.title: Snap spins off AI video team into new company, Dotmo, due to costs
tech-ai.summary: Snapchat parent Snap is spinning off its AI video development team into an independent company called Dotmo as the social media giant works to manage operational costs. Current Snap employees are leaving the company to focus full-time on AI-driven video technology development.

tech-ai.title: Rivian owners file lawsuit alleging false promises on self-driving features
tech-ai.summary: Rivian vehicle owners have filed a class-action lawsuit claiming the electric vehicle manufacturer falsely promised hands-free autonomous driving capabilities for first-generation R1 vehicles over several years. The litigation highlights ongoing consumer disputes over self-driving technology development timelines in the automotive industry.

tech-ai.title: The 11 standout startups from YC's Demo Day, according to VCs
tech-ai.summary: Venture capital investors have identified 11 notable startups from Y Combinator's Spring 2026 batch, with some commanding valuations exceeding $175 million. The selections reflect investor confidence in emerging companies across various technology sectors.

tech-ai.title: Telegram ban in India sparks a rush to VPNs, rival apps
tech-ai.summary: Telegram's ban in India has prompted users to turn to virtual private networks and alternative messaging platforms, as the messaging app argues authorities should target specific content rather than blocking the entire platform. The enforcement has triggered significant traffic to competing services and privacy-focused applications.

tech-ai.title: Almost half of US singles feel negatively about AI in dating, Match says
tech-ai.summary: According to dating platform Match, approximately 47 percent of American singles view the use of AI in dating negatively, though many users remain receptive to AI-assisted profile optimization and conversation suggestions. The data reveals mixed consumer sentiment toward artificial intelligence integration in romantic matchmaking services.

tech-ai.title: Amazon employees say they're facing termination for backing data center limits
tech-ai.summary: Three Amazon software engineers are accusing the company of retaliating against them for testifying in support of Seattle data center restrictions, citing violations of city employment discrimination laws protecting political speech. The dispute underscores growing internal conflicts at major technology companies over environmental and infrastructure policies.

world-news.title: US lifts naval blockade as Iran's supreme leader says Trump made deal 'out of desperation'
world-news.summary: The United States has lifted its naval blockade of Iran following a significant diplomatic agreement between Washington and Tehran to end their conflict. Iran's supreme leader has publicly disputed the deal's terms, claiming President Trump signed it under desperation, indicating ongoing tensions despite the accord.

world-news.title: What Iran and US get from deal and why both could struggle to keep it
world-news.summary: Analysts assess the competing claims of victory by Washington and Tehran following their war-ending agreement, examining the substantive gains and vulnerabilities for each party. The analysis suggests both nations face significant challenges in maintaining compliance with the accord's terms.

world-news.title: EU won't lift key Iran sanctions until formal nuclear deal reached
world-news.summary: The European Union has signaled it will maintain key sanctions against Iran pending completion of a formal nuclear agreement, complicating the broader diplomatic picture following the US-Iran conflict resolution. This stance underscores ongoing international divisions over Iran's nuclear program and regional role.

world-news.title: Hegseth renews Nato criticism and says US will review presence in Europe
world-news.summary: US Defense Secretary Hegseth has renewed criticism of NATO and announced that the United States will review its military presence and commitments across Europe. This statement reflects broader US policy shifts toward the alliance and signals potential reductions in American defense commitments on the continent.

world-news.title: Bowen: US-Iran deal raises inescapable question of what the war was for
world-news.summary: Analysis examines the strategic implications of the US-Iran peace agreement, questioning the ultimate purpose and outcome of the conflict while noting that the Iranian regime not only survived but has emerged strengthened from the war. The piece raises concerns about whether the agreed settlement justified the human costs incurred.

world-news.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
world-news.summary: Nearly 200 Ukrainian drones struck a major oil refinery and shopping center southeast of Moscow, causing fires and environmental concerns as residents report black rain in the aftermath. The attack represents one of the largest Ukrainian strikes against Russian infrastructure targets.

world-news.title: Zimbabwe bill to scrap presidential elections sparks backlash
world-news.summary: Zimbabwe's proposed legislation to eliminate presidential elections has triggered significant political controversy, with supporters framing it as governance reform while critics view it as a democratic threat and turning point for the nation's political trajectory. The bill represents a contested shift in the country's constitutional framework.

world-news.title: Thirty-five killed as gunmen attack Niger's biggest airport
world-news.summary: Armed gunmen attacked Niger's largest airport in an assault that killed 35 people, continuing a pattern of militant Islamist violence in the region. The attack marks the second strike on the airport facility within six months as Niger contends with an ongoing jihadist insurgency.

world-news.title: Iran war live: JD Vance delays trip to Switzerland for talks with Tehran
world-news.summary: US Vice President JD Vance postponed a Switzerland visit to focus on negotiations with Iran as a 60-day diplomatic window opens following the conflict resolution agreement. The delay underscores the prioritization of US-Iran negotiations in the immediate aftermath of the war's conclusion.

world-news.title: US judge orders release of Palestinian rights advocate detained by ICE
world-news.summary: A US federal judge has mandated the release of a mosque leader detained by Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ruling that his arrest and detention were improper based on his Palestine advocacy activities. The decision raises questions about the limits of immigration enforcement and First Amendment protections in the context of political speech.

finance-markets.title: Iran to get access to $6bn of frozen funds to buy US goods
finance-markets.summary: The US-Iran interim deal will release $6 billion in frozen Iranian funds held in Qatar, disbursed in phases to enable purchases of American goods. This significant capital repatriation marks a major financial component of the broader diplomatic accord between the Trump administration and Tehran.

finance-markets.title: SpaceX plots $20bn bond deal after record IPO
finance-markets.summary: SpaceX is preparing a $20 billion debt offering following its record $86 billion initial public offering, signaling aggressive capital markets activity by the artificial intelligence and aerospace company. The dual equity and debt strategy reflects strong investor appetite and the firm's expansion plans.

finance-markets.title: Oil Heads for Deep Weekly Loss as Hormuz Flows Start to Pick Up
finance-markets.summary: Crude oil prices are declining sharply as the US-Iran interim peace deal enables the resumption of normal shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, reversing the market's largest ever supply shock. The restoration of energy flows is expected to ease global inflation pressures and stabilize commodity markets.

finance-markets.title: Currency Traders Pile Into Dollar Call Options After Hawkish Fed
finance-markets.summary: Hedge funds and currency traders are increasing positions in dollar call options following the Federal Reserve's hawkish policy stance, betting on further appreciation amid expectations for sustained higher US interest rates. This positioning reflects market conviction in continued dollar strength.

finance-markets.title: Goldman Says UK's Sterling Now the Most Overvalued G10 Currency
finance-markets.summary: Goldman Sachs has identified sterling as the most overvalued major currency among G10 peers, following its recovery from post-Brexit lows, with the pound expected to face mounting downward pressure. This assessment signals potential currency volatility and trading opportunities in foreign exchange markets.

finance-markets.title: US-Iran accord opens way for Hormuz charges, industry warns
finance-markets.summary: Shipping executives have raised concerns that the US-Iran interim deal language may enable Tehran to impose tolls or establish a fund similar to the Strait of Malacca model after a 60-day window. This potential monetization of the crucial waterway could create new financial costs for global maritime trade.

finance-markets.title: EU set to remove barriers to banks' cross-border capital flows
finance-markets.summary: A European Commission draft report proposes dismantling regulatory obstacles to cross-border capital movements among EU banks, aimed at improving the competitive position of European lenders relative to their American counterparts. The initiative reflects efforts to strengthen the bloc's financial sector integration and efficiency.

finance-markets.title: India Short-Bond Rally Faces Risks From Cash Drain, Analysts Say
finance-markets.summary: A robust rally in India's short-end bond market, driven by efforts to attract foreign capital, may lose momentum as the central bank is expected to absorb excess liquidity from the financial system. Analysts warn that the cash drainage could undermine the bond market's recent performance gains.

finance-markets.title: East Asian Refiners Eye Fuel Sale Boost as Hormuz Reopens
finance-markets.summary: East Asian refineries are accelerating fuel exports after months of prioritizing domestic supply, seeking to capitalize on market conditions before normalized flows through the Strait of Hormuz arrive. This supply strategy reflects efforts to maximize margins amid the transition to restored global trade patterns.

finance-markets.title: MSCI Flags Indonesia Information Flow Concerns in Annual Review
finance-markets.summary: MSCI has raised concerns regarding investor access to timely information in Indonesia's equity markets during its annual review, signaling potential constraints ahead of a possible index downgrade. The information disclosure issues could further pressure Indonesia's already struggling stock market valuations.

science-space.title: Stages of Star Formation
science-space.summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has captured detailed imagery of the Orion Molecular Clouds, revealing every stage of star formation from stellar embryos to protoplanetary discs. The observations provide unprecedented insights into the complex processes that govern how stars are born from clouds of gas and dust.

science-space.title: NASA Mission to Study Space Weather Impacts of Earth's Atmosphere
science-space.summary: NASA has selected the DAPHNE (Dynamic Atmosphere-Ionosphere Explorer) mission concept to advance understanding of how space weather and atmospheric dynamics influence the space environment and affect critical technologies. The mission will move into Phase B development with the goal of improving prediction capabilities for impacts on GPS systems, satellites, and astronaut safety.

science-space.title: Astronomers solve the mystery of black holes' delayed cosmic 'burps'
science-space.summary: A new study explains why black holes emit massive radio outbursts years after consuming stars, providing astronomers with a chemical blueprint to predict these events earlier. This breakthrough advances understanding of black hole behavior and post-merger dynamics in the universe.

science-space.title: James Webb Space Telescope finds a salty surprise on famous 'Pink Planet'
science-space.summary: Using the James Webb Space Telescope, astronomers have discovered unexpected salt compounds on one of the coldest exoplanets ever discovered, designated the Pink Planet. This discovery provides new insights into atmospheric composition and chemistry on distant worlds.

science-space.title: NASA's Lucy Reveals Wobbling, Peanut-Shaped Asteroid
science-space.summary: NASA's Lucy spacecraft has revealed that asteroid Donaldjohanson is a complex, peanut-shaped body with significant rotational wobble, having undergone substantial activity since its formation 155 million years ago following a violent collision. The discovery demonstrates that even small asteroids possess intricate geological histories.

science-space.title: Scientists trace high-energy ghost particle to the 'Shadow Blaster' galaxy
science-space.summary: Researchers have potentially traced a high-energy neutrino to its source in the Shadow Blaster galaxy, which would represent the first direct link between an individual dusty star-forming galaxy and a high-energy neutrino event. This discovery could advance understanding of cosmic particle acceleration mechanisms.

science-space.title: NASA Testing Advanced Capabilities for Moon, Mars Rovers
science-space.summary: NASA's ERNEST rover prototype successfully completed a 16-mile autonomous test drive across challenging Colorado Desert terrain with minimal human intervention, demonstrating advanced robotic autonomy and mobility capabilities. These tests represent significant progress in developing rover technology for future lunar and Martian exploration missions.

science-space.title: Hubble Glimpses Merging Galaxy Clusters
science-space.summary: The Hubble Space Telescope has imaged galaxy cluster CL0016+1609 (MACS J0018.5+1626), revealing it to be two distinct clusters merging along Earth's line of sight. X-ray observations of this system provide valuable insights into the dynamics and composition of merging galaxy clusters.

science-space.title: 'It's quite a bit more than we expected': Satellite reveals immense scale of GPS signal tampering
science-space.summary: An experimental satellite has mapped the unprecedented scale of GPS jamming across Europe and the Middle East, revealing signal tampering to be more widespread than previously documented. This discovery highlights emerging threats to critical positioning and navigation infrastructure.

science-space.title: NASA Awards Contract for Commercial Satellite Data Acquisition
science-space.summary: NASA has selected eight new commercial companies and renewed partnerships with six existing providers to expand access to commercial satellite data for researchers and decision-makers. These acquisitions will enhance NASA's Earth observation capabilities with high-resolution and frequent measurements from commercial sources.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Foxconn and EMP to begin producing electric SUVs in Poland from 2029
automotive-ev-battery.summary: ElectroMobility Poland and Foxconn have confirmed plans to establish an electric vehicle manufacturing facility in Jaworzno, southern Poland, with production commencing in 2029. The plant is designed to produce up to 400,000 electric SUVs annually, representing a significant €715 million investment in European EV manufacturing capacity.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Hyundai establishes battery assembly for the Ioniq 3 in Turkey
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Hyundai is expanding its Turkish production facility in İzmit to include onsite battery pack assembly for the Ioniq 3 as part of a €715 million investment program. The automated battery assembly operation will enhance the manufacturer's vertical integration and supply chain resilience in Europe.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Electrify America opens 20-bay charging hub in Santa Barbara
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Electrify America has inaugurated a large-format charging station in downtown Santa Barbara featuring 20 charging bays with up to 350 kW power output and a 1.9 MW battery energy storage system. This represents the company's largest integrated energy storage deployment to date and advances high-capacity public charging infrastructure.

automotive-ev-battery.title: The Mobility House, EcoG and EV-Tech unveil BiDi Wallbox
automotive-ev-battery.summary: German companies The Mobility House and EcoG, in partnership with Chinese manufacturer EV-Tech, have developed a bidirectional DC wallbox supporting Vehicle-to-Grid and Vehicle-to-Home functionality. The technology aims to democratize bidirectional charging capabilities for mass-market adoption.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Germany: World's oldest brewery launches charging hub for electric trucks
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The Bavarian State Brewery Weihenstephan and Delta Charge have opened a fully electrified charging depot for battery-electric trucks at the brewery's Freising logistics center. The facility integrates fast charging, battery storage, and solar power to support fleet electrification while reducing operational energy costs.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Char.gy to deliver 1000 new EV chargepoints in Enfield
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The London Borough of Enfield will receive 1,000 new EV charging points through a partnership with Char.gy, with the majority installed as lamp post retrofits and additional standalone chargers at five borough locations. This deployment represents a significant expansion of public charging infrastructure in the London area.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Nemo project presents smart BMS for electric vehicles
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A research consortium including TU Graz has developed an advanced battery management system under the EU-funded Nemo project that uses new sensors and algorithms to provide deeper insight into battery behavior. The technology is designed to enhance both electric vehicle safety and battery service life.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Kia EV5 review: Not the fastest, but one of the most impressive
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kia's new EV5 compact SUV demonstrates practical engineering focused on delivering real-world value rather than headline specifications in one of Europe's largest vehicle segments. The 4.61-meter model emphasizes balanced performance and usability across key consumer priorities.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Mahle chargeBIG unveils new AC charging station with four charging points
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mahle chargeBIG has unveiled a new AC charging terminal developed with Langmatz Energy that can simultaneously supply up to four electric vehicles with 22 kW of charging power each. The innovation addresses mid-tier charging infrastructure needs for residential and commercial applications.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD Datang Flagship SUV Launch Impresses
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD has officially launched the Datang premium SUV with over 150,000 pre-orders, featuring the company's second-generation Blade Battery technology. The successful launch demonstrates strong market demand for BYD's latest battery innovations, though supply constraints remain a consideration.

health-biotech.title: FDA advisory panel endorses Moderna mRNA flu vaccine that was subject of controversy
health-biotech.summary: An FDA advisory panel has unanimously endorsed Moderna's seasonal mRNA flu vaccine, clearing a significant regulatory hurdle for the innovative immunization approach. The endorsement resolves earlier controversy surrounding the vaccine candidate and advances it toward potential market authorization.

health-biotech.title: Ebola cases increase almost 40% in a week as death toll passes 200
health-biotech.summary: The ongoing Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda has escalated sharply, with cases rising nearly 40 percent in a single week as fatalities exceed 200. The rapid surge underscores the ongoing public health threat in the affected regions.

health-biotech.title: Shingles vaccine may lower dementia risk, new study finds
health-biotech.summary: A new study demonstrates that nursing home residents receiving at least one dose of shingles vaccine showed a 24 percent reduction in dementia development. The findings suggest an unexpected cognitive benefit beyond the vaccine's established efficacy against herpes zoster infection.

health-biotech.title: IRA's Medicare out-of-pocket cap increased access to drugs, study finds
health-biotech.summary: A new study published in JAMA Health Forum shows that the Medicare annual out-of-pocket cost cap implemented in January 2025 significantly increased medication usage, particularly for expensive drugs. The findings demonstrate the policy's early success in improving drug access for beneficiaries.

health-biotech.title: Novocure's electric fields therapy fails key glioblastoma study
health-biotech.summary: Novocure's experimental electric fields device failed to extend survival in glioblastoma patients when combined with chemoradiation in a Phase 3 trial. The disappointing results dampen the company's expansion prospects for the technology in brain cancer treatment.

health-biotech.title: Eli Lilly ends 340B drug discounts to some hospitals for failing to provide claims data
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly has begun enforcing its threat to eliminate federally mandated price breaks to a handful of hospitals in the 340B discount program that failed to provide required claims data. The move escalates tensions between pharmaceutical manufacturers and healthcare providers over program transparency and compliance.

health-biotech.title: How many Americans can afford high-quality health care? A new poll finds the number has fallen
health-biotech.summary: A recent poll reveals that only approximately half of U.S. adults report being able to afford quality healthcare with adequate access. The finding underscores growing affordability challenges facing the American population.

health-biotech.title: CMS recalculates Medicare Advantage star ratings again, adding more volatility to program
health-biotech.summary: The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services has recalculated Medicare Advantage plan star ratings, creating additional uncertainty in a program where bonus payments have reached $16 billion annually. The volatility in ratings poses challenges for plan stability and consumer decision-making.

health-biotech.title: Treasury weighs screening investments in China biotech
health-biotech.summary: U.S. Treasury Department officials are evaluating potential restrictions on American investments in Chinese biotech companies as part of broader national security considerations. The discussions reflect growing scrutiny of cross-border biotechnology capital flows.

health-biotech.title: Moderna adds commercial chief, expands Stephen Hoge's role; Reunion Neuroscience CEO departs
health-biotech.summary: Moderna has appointed Ester Banque as chief commercial officer and expanded the responsibilities of president Stephen Hoge in preparation for upcoming product launches. The organizational changes signal the company's strategic positioning for future commercial opportunities.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. agencies seek stablecoin customer-ID rules akin to banks in new GENIUS Act pitch
crypto-web3.summary: The Federal Reserve, Treasury and other U.S. regulators have proposed new identification standards for stablecoin customers through the GENIUS Act, now open for public comment. This represents a significant regulatory framework that would bring stablecoin issuers closer to traditional banking compliance standards.

crypto-web3.title: JPMorgan says bitcoin mining economics have 'worsened' as BTC trades below production cost
crypto-web3.summary: JPMorgan estimates the current production cost of bitcoin at approximately $78,000, while the cryptocurrency currently trades around $62,500, indicating deteriorating mining economics. This significant gap between production costs and market price suggests challenges for bitcoin miners' profitability.

crypto-web3.title: Ex-Celsius CEO Mashinsky gets U.S. CFTC ban in final resolution with regulator
crypto-web3.summary: Alexander Mashinsky, founder of failed crypto lender Celsius, has been formally banned from CFTC registration as part of a final regulatory settlement. The ban follows Mashinsky's earlier imprisonment for fraud charges related to the collapse of the lending platform.

crypto-web3.title: Algorand unveils roadmap to achieve quantum resistance by 2028
crypto-web3.summary: Algorand has announced a strategic roadmap to implement quantum-resistant cryptography by 2028, reflecting the broader crypto industry's recognition that such upgrades require years of development. The transition will necessitate changes to user wallets and core protocol infrastructure across the network.

crypto-web3.title: Ethereum Foundation loses another key leader as co-executive director Hsiao-Wei Wang resigns
crypto-web3.summary: Hsiao-Wei Wang, co-executive director of the Ethereum Foundation, has resigned in what marks the latest high-profile departure from the organization. Wang's exit follows the recent resignation of fellow co-executive director Tomasz Stańczak, raising questions about leadership stability at the foundation.

crypto-web3.title: Alchemy's AI-driven identity and payment service gains access to Visa network
crypto-web3.summary: Alchemy's AgentCard has been integrated with Visa Intelligent Commerce, enabling AI agents from any provider to conduct commercial transactions on the Visa network. This development represents a significant step in connecting decentralized AI infrastructure with traditional payment systems.

crypto-web3.title: Fidelity launches GENIUS-aligned money market fund for stablecoin issuers
crypto-web3.summary: Fidelity has launched a money market fund that complies with the GENIUS Act framework, investing exclusively in reserve assets permitted for stablecoin issuers. This product demonstrates institutional adoption of the regulatory standards being established for stablecoin infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: New Texas power grid allocation framework could lift bitcoin miners turned data center operators
crypto-web3.summary: The Electric Reliability Council of Texas has introduced a new power grid allocation framework that could benefit bitcoin miners transitioning to data center operations, as data centers account for nearly 90% of the state's large-load demand. This policy shift reflects the growing convergence of crypto mining infrastructure with AI and data center operations.

crypto-web3.title: CryptoQuant says bitcoin microtransactions now make up 80% of daily transactions
crypto-web3.summary: CryptoQuant reports that bitcoin transactions below 0.01 BTC now comprise approximately 80% of daily transactions, a substantial increase from 44% in 2023. This shift suggests growing usage of bitcoin for smaller payments and transactions.

crypto-web3.title: Sen. Gillibrand's son is building a perps exchange that wants dual CFTC, SEC oversight: report
crypto-web3.summary: The American Perpetuals Exchange Corporation, led by Senator Gillibrand's son, has raised $30 million at a $300 million valuation and is seeking dual regulatory oversight from both the CFTC and SEC. This development highlights growing interest in launching regulated derivatives platforms with comprehensive regulatory frameworks.

cybersecurity.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
cybersecurity.summary: A contractor for the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and classified agency secrets on a public GitHub account, triggering congressional demands for accountability. CISA continues efforts to contain the breach and invalidate the compromised credentials, raising significant questions about internal security practices at a federal cybersecurity agency.

cybersecurity.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released approximately 200 security updates across Windows and supported software, marking the largest monthly patch cycle in company history. Nearly three dozen vulnerabilities received critical severity ratings, with exploit code already publicly available for at least three of the flaws.

cybersecurity.title: 'Popa' Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers have connected the Popa Android botnet, which has compromised millions of consumer TV boxes over four years for advertising fraud and data scraping, to NetNut, a residential proxy service operated by publicly-traded Israeli company Alarum Technologies Ltd. The discovery links a major cybercriminal infrastructure operation to legitimate corporate operations.

cybersecurity.title: INC Ransomware Emerges as Major RaaS Threat in 2026 with 830+ Victims Since 2023
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have documented INC's evolution from a fledgling ransomware-as-a-service operation into one of 2026's most prolific cybercrime groups, with over 830 confirmed victims since August 2023. The group has capitalized on opportunities created by the disruption of LockBit and BlackCat, absorbing affiliates and expanding its operational footprint.

cybersecurity.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
cybersecurity.summary: Dutch authorities arrested two co-owners of hosting companies that provided infrastructure for Russian-backed cyberattacks, disinformation campaigns, and influence operations targeting the European Union. The seizure of 800 servers targeted technical infrastructure previously linked to sanctioned providers facilitating malicious cyber activity.

cybersecurity.title: F5 Patches Two Critical NGINX Open Source Flaws Enabling Remote Code Execution
cybersecurity.summary: F5 released security updates addressing two critical vulnerabilities in NGINX Open Source, including a use-after-free flaw with a CVSS score of 9.2 that could be remotely exploited for code execution. The vulnerabilities affect widely deployed web server infrastructure used across enterprises globally.

cybersecurity.title: DragonForce Hackers Abuse Microsoft Teams Relays to Hide Backdoor.Turn C2 Traffic
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors associated with the DragonForce ransomware group have deployed a custom Go-based remote access trojan called Backdoor.Turn that conceals command-and-control communications within Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure. The backdoor was deployed against a major U.S. services firm, demonstrating sophisticated abuse of legitimate enterprise communication platforms.

cybersecurity.title: Salesforce Data Thefts Continue via Klue App Compromise
cybersecurity.summary: Klue's Battlecards application has been compromised to steal customer Salesforce data, marking the third integrated Salesforce application breach used for data theft. Notable victims include Huntress, a prominent cybersecurity vendor, highlighting risks in third-party application ecosystems.

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Details Windows Clipper Malware Campaign Using USB LNK Worm and Tor-Based C2
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft has disclosed a cryptocurrency clipper campaign active since February 2026 that employs Windows Script Host and ActiveX logic to launch Tor proxies and communicate with hidden-service command-and-control servers. The campaign demonstrates sophisticated malware techniques targeting cryptocurrency theft.

cybersecurity.title: The Scripts on Your Checkout Page Are Now a PCI DSS Problem
cybersecurity.summary: Third-party scripts loaded on modern e-commerce checkout pages—including analytics, tag managers, and support widgets—have become a compliance liability under updated PCI DSS requirements. A qualified security assessor confirmed that uncontrolled third-party code on payment pages creates data security risks that organizations must now actively manage.

startups-vc.title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
startups-vc.summary: Baseten, an AI inference infrastructure startup, is reportedly finalizing a $1.5 billion funding round at a $13 billion valuation, demonstrating continued investor appetite in the competitive AI inference market. The round underscores the broader "inference gold rush" as enterprises scale AI deployments.

startups-vc.title: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI for up to $85M
startups-vc.summary: Enterprise software company Elastic has agreed to acquire DeductiveAI, a three-year-old AI-powered bug detection startup, for up to $85 million. The acquisition demonstrates continued M&A activity in the AI tooling sector and Elastic's strategy to expand its software intelligence capabilities.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: World-Model Startup Odyssey Leads With $310M In Slower Week For Large Deals
startups-vc.summary: This week saw notable funding activity across diverse sectors including AI, fintech, quantum computing, biotech, and cybersecurity, with world-model startup Odyssey leading with a $310 million round. The mixed portfolio of large rounds reflects investor diversification beyond narrow AI verticals.

startups-vc.title: The AI Startup Funding Boom Is Not A Global Phenomenon
startups-vc.summary: U.S. companies have captured nearly 80% of global seed through growth-stage AI funding in 2026, marking a significant shift from pre-AI boom years when American companies typically secured less than half of all investment. The concentration reflects geographic disparities in AI infrastructure, talent, and capital access.

startups-vc.title: The 11 standout startups from YC's Demo Day, according to VCs
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator's Spring 2026 batch produced multiple standout startups commanding valuations exceeding $175 million, according to investor surveys conducted by TechCrunch. The strong valuations highlight continued VC enthusiasm for YC-backed founders despite broader market caution.

startups-vc.title: Rewriting Your Pitch: SaaS Isn't Dead, But The Playbook For Founders Is Changing
startups-vc.summary: The traditional SaaS fundraising playbook is evolving as AI and LLMs reshape the software market, pushing founders to focus on measurable business outcomes, defensible workflows, and outcome-based pricing models rather than software features alone. Successful founders must build deep competitive moats and demonstrate lasting customer value beyond AI capabilities.

startups-vc.title: AT&T Ventures' Head Vikram Taneja On The New Rules of Seed-Stage Defensibility
startups-vc.summary: AT&T Ventures head Vikram Taneja argues that while AI has lowered the barrier to building software, it has simultaneously shifted seed-stage technical risk assessment and redefined what constitutes defensible competitive advantages. Investors must now evaluate technical moats differently in an AI-saturated market.

startups-vc.title: The Boardroom Blind Spot: When Success Hides Disruption
startups-vc.summary: Corporate boards should proactively evaluate disruptive technologies like AI and quantum computing rather than waiting for performance deterioration, according to strategic adviser Itay Sagie. Companies must challenge successful business models while thriving to understand how technology-driven competitors could disrupt their markets.

startups-vc.title: Christopher Golda and Grey Baker Join YC as General Partners
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator announced the addition of Christopher Golda and Grey Baker as General Partners, expanding the accelerator's investment team. The appointments reflect YC's continued growth and commitment to supporting its portfolio companies across ecosystem development.

startups-vc.title: Artisan's Ava 2.0: What a Fully Autonomous AI BDR Actually Looks Like in Production with CEO Jaspar Carmichael-Jack
startups-vc.summary: Artisan CEO Jaspar Carmichael-Jack demonstrated Ava 2.0, a fully autonomous AI business development representative, at SaaStr AI Annual with production-ready capabilities that moved beyond typical founder demonstrations. The platform represents tangible progress in AI-driven sales automation, a critical growth area for enterprise software companies.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: Solar overtakes gas power in Asia for first time ever
energy-climate.summary: Solar power has surpassed natural gas to become Asia's third-largest electricity source, marking a significant milestone in the continent's energy transition. This shift reflects accelerating renewable energy deployment across the region and demonstrates the declining role of fossil fuels in Asia's power mix.

energy-climate.title: Q&A: What do China's provincial five-year plans say about climate and energy?
energy-climate.summary: China's provincial governments have published their 15th five-year plans, providing insight into climate and energy policy implementation at the subnational level. These plans reveal China's commitment to meeting national climate targets through coordinated provincial action.

energy-climate.title: India may exceed 60 GW of solar cell capacity by end of Q1 2027
energy-climate.summary: India's domestic solar cell manufacturing capacity is expected to reach approximately 60 GW by early 2027, driven by government localization policies and industry expansion. While this growth addresses supply chain resilience, analysts warn that rapid capacity buildout could pressure profitability and equipment utilization rates.

energy-climate.title: Argentina advances 232 battery bids in AlmaSADI tender
energy-climate.summary: Argentina's AlmaSADI battery storage tender has advanced 232 bids totaling 8.3 GW, nearly 12 times the original 700 MW target. The strong response demonstrates significant market interest in energy storage solutions across Latin America.

energy-climate.title: IPVF, TU Delft achieve 31% efficiency for 4 cm² perovskite-silicon tandem solar cell
energy-climate.summary: Researchers have achieved 31% efficiency on a perovskite-silicon tandem solar cell, representing a significant advancement in next-generation photovoltaic technology. This breakthrough demonstrates progress toward commercializing higher-efficiency solar cells that could improve renewable energy generation capacity.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: UK's EV drivers are now saving £1,100 each a year – and £3bn in total
energy-climate.summary: UK electric vehicle drivers are collectively saving approximately £3 billion annually through reduced fuel and maintenance costs, with individual drivers saving around £1,100 per year compared to petrol vehicles. This analysis comes amid government discussions regarding potential revisions to the UK's EV targets.

energy-climate.title: FRV secures 2.3GW of grid capacity for German renewable projects
energy-climate.summary: Spanish renewable energy developer Fotowatio Renewable Ventures has secured grid capacity for 2.3 GW of planned renewable and battery storage projects in Germany. This agreement supports Germany's continued expansion of distributed renewable energy infrastructure.

energy-climate.title: Cadeler completes installation of 100 turbines at Sofia Wind Farm
energy-climate.summary: Cadeler has finished installing all 100 Siemens Gamesa wind turbines at RWE's 1.4 GW Sofia offshore wind farm, marking completion of a major offshore wind installation project. This milestone represents significant progress in expanding European offshore wind capacity.

energy-climate.title: Oxford PV achieves 25.6% efficiency for perovskite-silicon tandem module based on shingled design
energy-climate.summary: Oxford PV has demonstrated 25.6% efficiency in a perovskite-silicon tandem solar module using an advanced shingled design that reduces losses and improves performance. This development advances the commercialization of high-efficiency tandem solar technology for utility-scale applications.

energy-climate.title: Portugal plans capacity market with dedicated battery storage auction
energy-climate.summary: Portugal is establishing a capacity market framework that includes dedicated battery storage auctions, with plans to procure 750 MVA of storage capacity. This policy initiative strengthens grid stability and supports the integration of variable renewable energy sources.

asia-pacific.title: Xi's Authoritarian Blueprint for the Myanmar Junta
asia-pacific.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping has signaled to Myanmar's military leader Min Aung Hlaing that military force alone cannot maintain control, implying instead that adopting Chinese Communist Party-style totalitarian governance mechanisms would be more effective in pacifying the population. This represents a significant geopolitical development in China's influence over Myanmar's governance trajectory during an ongoing period of military rule and public resistance.

asia-pacific.title: US-Iran talks in Switzerland delayed, White House says Vance remains ready to go
asia-pacific.summary: Plans for US Vice-President J.D. Vance to travel to Switzerland for technical talks with Iran have been postponed, with the White House citing ongoing logistical challenges in finalizing negotiations. The delay reflects the continued complexity of US-Iran diplomatic efforts amid broader Middle Eastern tensions.

asia-pacific.title: Wang Yi's Visit and Mongolia's Foreign Policy Balancing Act
asia-pacific.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi's visit to Mongolia occurs amid the country's hosting of a United Nations desertification conference and its implementation of a 'third neighbor' foreign policy strategy. The timing underscores Mongolia's delicate diplomatic balance between great power interests while pursuing independent multilateral engagement.

asia-pacific.title: India's 5G Fighter Aircraft Dilemma
asia-pacific.summary: Russia has offered India joint production of the SU-57 fifth-generation fighter aircraft at a time when India is advancing its own Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) indigenous development program. This strategic offer presents India with a significant military procurement decision amid broader defense modernization and geopolitical realignment considerations.

asia-pacific.title: No Reset at G7: Trump Was Trump, Modi Was Not Modi
asia-pacific.summary: The Modi-Trump bilateral meeting at the G7 revealed constraints on India's willingness to engage in direct moral criticism of US policies when faced with American diplomatic pressure. The interaction highlighted tensions between India's stated values and its pragmatic engagement with great power relationships.

asia-pacific.title: The USMCA Review Will Be a China (and Asia) Policy Test for Mexico
asia-pacific.summary: Mexico faces pressure from Washington during the renegotiation of the USMCA trade agreement to demonstrate that it is not serving as a conduit for Chinese economic penetration, while Mexico's manufacturing sector remains structurally dependent on Asian inputs. This tension reflects broader US-Asia-Latin America trade dynamics and regional supply chain vulnerabilities.

asia-pacific.title: Chinese AI improves forecasts as Hong Kong braces for super typhoons
asia-pacific.summary: A newly deployed artificial intelligence system at the Hong Kong Observatory and China's National Meteorological Centre significantly enhances the accuracy of typhoon intensification predictions, demonstrating effectiveness during Typhoon Jangmi tracking. The technological advancement addresses a critical gap in regional weather forecasting capabilities.

asia-pacific.title: The Chinese scientists hoping to use a space solar plant to power the world
asia-pacific.summary: Researchers at Xidian University in Shaanxi province are developing space-based solar power technology capable of transmitting energy over vast distances via microwave transmission, with successful preliminary tests demonstrating power transmission over 100 meters. This represents a significant step toward a potentially transformative clean energy technology with global applications.

asia-pacific.title: On the Bangladesh-India Border, BJP Victories Spike Anxieties
asia-pacific.summary: Following Bharatiya Janata Party electoral victories in West Bengal, minority communities including Muslims and Rohingyas in border regions are experiencing heightened anxieties about their security and future status. The situation reflects broader concerns about communal tensions and minority protections in eastern India.

asia-pacific.title: Central Asia's Relations With Taliban-ruled Afghanistan Continue to Deepen
asia-pacific.summary: The inaugural Afghanistan-Central Asia Consultative Dialogue held in Kabul in April signals a significant deepening of diplomatic and economic relations between the Taliban-led government and neighboring Central Asian states over the past five years. The development indicates growing regional integration and normalization of Afghanistan's international standing despite international isolation.
